steps.title: Compare the spreads
steps.content: Company A's values are clustered in a narrow range, about $25$ to $40$ thousand units. Company B's values spread across a much wider range, about $15$ to $50$ thousand units.

steps.title: Use the meaning of standard deviation
steps.content: Standard deviation measures how spread out the data values are from the mean. A narrow cluster has a smaller standard deviation, while a wider spread has a larger standard deviation.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Company A has the smaller spread, so its standard deviation is less than Company B's. This matches choice A.
